heading: Why People Want to Remove Watermarks
text: Watermarks are placed on images for a reason: they protect the creator's intellectual property and signal that the image is not free to use without permission. Stock photo agencies watermark preview images to let buyers evaluate content before purchasing. Photographers watermark client proofs to prevent unpaid use.

heading: Legal Implications of Watermark Removal
text: In most jurisdictions, removing a watermark from an image you do not own is illegal. In the United States, the Digital Millennium Copyright Act specifically prohibits removing copyright management information, which includes watermarks. Violations can result in statutory damages of $2,500 to $25,000 per instance.

heading: Legitimate Alternatives to Watermark Removal
text: The simplest legitimate approach is to purchase the image. Stock photo agencies like Shutterstock, Adobe Stock, and iStock offer individual image licenses and subscription plans. Purchasing a license gives you a clean, high-resolution image without any watermark, plus legal protection to use it in your projects.

heading: Adding Your Own Watermarks with ToolPic
text: Instead of removing others' watermarks, protect your own work by adding watermarks to your images. Open ToolPic's Image Watermark tool to add text or logo watermarks to your photos before sharing them online. This ensures your work carries your identity wherever it travels on the internet.
